_meta.notes: Human homeland. Inland river basin city; districts are organized by craft, not by class. The four sub-population districts (weavers, smiths, farmers, scribes) each have rigid apprenticeship rules. The Furrow runs north-south through the city; its banks are public common ground that all four crafts share.

tagline: A city of single-craft compounds. Apprentices age seven; masters age thirty-five.
ambientPool: The looms speak louder than the people. Always have.
Cold smithing makes its own kind of music — clean, slow, no scream of red metal.
A barge of grain comes downriver from Furrowmouth.
Pageborn scribes carry stacks of clean orcish paper to and from the riverside.
Every adult here is the master of one thing and only one thing. They look almost embarrassed about it.

rooms.summary: Open square ringed by the four largest weaving compounds. The clack of looms reaches the square through every doorway. Bolts of named cloth hang outside the gates — each cloth has its own name, like a person.
ambientPool: A bolt named 'Late October' is rolled out across the square for inspection. The light catches it differently from any other.
An apprentice of nine carries a thread spool across the square at a careful pace.
A master weaver of forty-one rests on a bench. They look very tired. They are getting close.
Across the square, a Coldhammer's apprentice runs a message, dodges three weavers, makes it through.

exits.narrative: East to the smith district where the cold forges work.
South to the river commons where every craft mingles.
North to the scribe quarter, where the orcish paper arrives.

rooms.summary: Coldhammer compound. The forges are cold — banked low, just warm. Smiths shape the iron at room temperature, hammering it slowly into shape. The technique is impossible everywhere else; the humid air of Threadwarden is the only place it works.
ambientPool: An iron blank rings clear under a steady cold strike.
A master smith of forty-eight sits on a stool, watching their journeyman, saying nothing for an hour.
An apprentice of twelve makes their first true cold-strike and the master nods, just once.
From the next street the looms continue, indifferent.

exits.narrative: West back to the loom square.

rooms.summary: Pageborn district. The only humans who learn another language; their orcish is good enough that the Long Indices respects it. Stacks of clean paper are piled in window-sills to air.
ambientPool: A Pageborn scribe practices an orcish glyph fifty times in a row, each one slightly more correct.
Three of the older Pageborn argue in low voices about whether a translation honors the source.
A bell rings and a Pageborn child runs out to receive a paper shipment.
Through an open window: the sound of an orcish reading-rhythm, very faint.

exits.narrative: South back to the loom square.

rooms.summary: Public riverbank common. The only ground in the city not owned by a craft. Children of every district come here. Apprentices flirt across the trades.
ambientPool: A weaver's apprentice and a smith's apprentice share a sandwich on the bank.
A Furrowfolk farmer ties a flat barge to a post and waits. The wheat will be unloaded when somebody comes for it.
The river is not deep here. Children wade and shout.
A master who is only a few seasons from teaching looks at the water and is quiet.
